name: Funding — SEAP Approved-Provider Shortlist (WA Bellingham)
outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/seap_shortlist.md exists with: (a) at least 5 WA ESD-approved SEAP training providers serving Whatcom County or available to Bellingham residents, (b) each row has provider name, phone, email or web contact, minimum required contact hours, format (online async / online live / in-person), and enrollment lead time, (c) ranked ascending by minimum contact hours with explicit recommended call order, (d) async-friendly providers flagged for solo founder eligibility.
anti_requirements: Do NOT invent providers or hour counts — every row must trace to a cited source URL.
Do NOT include providers that explicitly do not accept Whatcom County / Bellingham residents.
Do NOT recommend a provider based on training quality — the ranking criterion is minimum contact hours.
Do NOT submit any application or contact any provider on behalf of the operator — research only.

jobs.label: discover_authority_source
jobs.description: Locate the authoritative WA ESD list of SEAP-approved training providers
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/00_source.md identifies the canonical ESD URL hosting the SEAP-approved-provider list and the as-of date of the listing.
jobs.prompt:
Find the authoritative Washington Employment Security Department (ESD) source for the current list of SEAP-approved training providers. Start at www.esd.wa.gov/training-programs.

Write `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/00_source.md` with:
- **Source URL** (the canonical page that lists SEAP-approved providers)
- **As-of date** (when the list was last updated, if surfaced)
- **Format** (HTML page, PDF, embedded list, etc.)
- **Notes** (any caveats — e.g. list is filtered by region, or provider list is gated behind a form)

If the list is split across multiple pages, identify all of them. If you cannot find an authoritative ESD-hosted list, say so explicitly and stop — do not fall back to third-party aggregators.

jobs.label: extract_provider_inventory
jobs.description: Extract the full structured list of approved SEAP providers from the authoritative source
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/01_inventory.md contains a markdown table of every approved provider with name, region/county served, primary contact, and source-line reference.
jobs.prompt:
Read `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/00_source.md` and pull the full provider inventory from the source URL identified there.

Write `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/01_inventory.md` as a markdown table with columns:
| Provider | Region/County | Primary Contact | Source line |

One row per approved provider. Do not filter at this stage — extract everything the source lists. If any field is unavailable from the source, write `(not listed)` rather than inventing.

jobs.label: filter_to_bellingham_eligible
jobs.description: Filter inventory to providers eligible for a Bellingham WA / Whatcom County resident
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/02_eligible.md contains the subset of providers a Bellingham WA resident can enroll with, with eligibility reasoning per row.
jobs.prompt:
Read `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/01_inventory.md`. Keep only providers that meet at least ONE of:
- Explicitly serve Whatcom County or Bellingham WA
- Statewide / serve all WA residents
- Online-only / location-agnostic delivery

Write `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/02_eligible.md` as a table with columns:
| Provider | Eligibility basis | Source |

For each kept row, the **Eligibility basis** must cite a specific phrase or service-area note from the source.

jobs.label: enrich_provider_details
jobs.description: For each eligible provider, find phone, email, minimum contact hours, format, enrollment lead time
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/03_enriched.md adds per-provider rows with phone, email/web, minimum contact hours, format (online async / online live / in-person), and enrollment lead time.
jobs.prompt:
Read `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/02_eligible.md`. For each eligible provider, search the provider's own website for:
- **Phone**
- **Email or web contact form URL**
- **Minimum required contact hours** for SEAP-qualifying training (most providers publish a program length; if SEAP-specific hours are not published, note the program's total contact hours)
- **Format**: `online_async` / `online_live` / `in_person` / `hybrid`
- **Enrollment lead time**: rolling enrollment, monthly cohorts, fixed start dates, etc.
- **Source URL** for each fact

Write `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/03_enriched.md` as a markdown table. If a field is unavailable, write `(unknown)` and note in a footer what would be needed to resolve it (e.g. "must call provider").

Do not invent contact info. Verify phone numbers by surfacing the page that displays them.

jobs.label: compose_shortlist
jobs.description: Produce the final ranked shortlist with recommended call order
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/seap_shortlist.md is the operator-facing deliverable: top 5 providers ranked by minimum contact hours, with phone, email, format, enrollment lead time, and a recommended call order.
jobs.prompt:
Read `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/03_enriched.md`. Produce the final shortlist at `artifacts/funding/seap_shortlist_20260501/seap_shortlist.md`.

Structure:

1. **Operator context** (one paragraph: solo tech founder in Bellingham WA, currently on UI, building Praxis as the self-employment venture, optimizing for minimum contact hours)
2. **Top 5 providers** (ranked ascending by minimum contact hours; tie-break by enrollment lead time, then by online-async availability)
   - For each: name, phone, email/web, minimum hours, format, lead time, async-friendly flag (yes/no), one-line note on what to ask in the intake call
3. **Recommended call order** (numbered list; one sentence per provider explaining why it is in this slot)
4. **Open questions** (anything that requires the operator to call to resolve)
5. **Disqualified providers and why** (short list — providers from `02_eligible.md` that did not make the top 5, with one-line reason)

Be opinionated about the call order. Hedge only where the data genuinely splits.
